articles.headline: CBI charge sheet in NEET UG leak may not name officials
articles.summary: The CBI has concluded its probe into the NEET UG 2026 paper leak, finding no involvement of government officials or NTA office bearers. The first charge sheet will focus on private individuals, including three teachers from Latur. Investigations determined that question papers were stolen by teachers at a contract center familiar with the set-up, rather than through a systemic breach.

articles.headline: Iran talks to go on, but truce over: Trump
articles.summary: Former US President Donald Trump stated that while talks between the US and Iran will continue, the ceasefire between the two nations is officially over. This follows mysterious airstrikes hitting Iranian military sites. Trump warned that the US will respond to any Iranian aggression against its interests, despite ongoing diplomatic efforts in Switzerland to de-escalate regional tensions.

articles.headline: Centre plans standards for messaging apps
articles.summary: The Indian government is moving to implement uniform standards for messaging platforms like WhatsApp and Telegram to curb online fraud and anonymous threats. Key focus areas include standardizing user identification features and 'usernames.' The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) aims to prevent anonymity from being used to fuel financial scams and harassment.

articles.headline: Borewell meters must, will decide Delhi water bills
articles.summary: The Delhi government has mandated the installation of water meters for all domestic and commercial borewells in the city. The move aims to regulate groundwater extraction and ensure billing is based on actual consumption rather than fixed rates. Officials stated that this policy is essential for groundwater conservation and managing the capital's water crisis under a new borewell policy.

articles.headline: 17-year-old NDA cadet dies during routine PT
articles.summary: A first-term cadet at the National Defence Academy (NDA) in Pune died after losing consciousness during a routine physical training session. Despite immediate medical assistance, he could not be revived. The NDA has ordered a Court of Inquiry (CoI) to determine the cause of death. This is the third such incident at the academy since October 2025.

articles.headline: Vijay gives job letters to Karur stampede victims
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu chief minister Vijay distributed government job appointment letters to the kin of those killed in the 2025 Karur stampede. During his visit, the first since assuming office, Vijay also announced a proposed memorial for the victims. He used the occasion to criticize the opposition DMK for allegedly exploiting the tragedy for political mileage.

articles.headline: BJP’s Bihar bypoll candidate pulls out from contest
articles.summary: Abhishek Kumar Sinha, the BJP's nominee for the Bankipur assembly bypoll in Bihar, has withdrawn his candidacy citing 'family reasons.' The sudden withdrawal forced the party to quickly name Neeraj Yadav as the new candidate. The development comes just before the July 30 polls, causing minor disruption to the party's local campaign strategy.

articles.headline: Death penalty to man for killing 1-yr-old nephew
articles.summary: A court in Firozabad, Uttar Pradesh, sentenced 24-year-old Jiten-dra Pathak to death for the murder of his 18-month-old nephew. The court described the act of slamming the child against the ground as 'heinous' and 'gruesome.' The motive was reportedly revenge against the child's father for rejecting Pathak's marriage proposal to his sister-in-law.

articles.headline: BCCI to review T20 showing vs Irl, Eng
articles.summary: The BCCI has scheduled a review meeting on July 19 to address India's recent T20 series losses against Ireland and England. The board aims to evaluate the performance of the core team members and discuss potential strategic changes ahead of the next international cycle. Secretary Jay Shah noted that the review is standard procedure and not aimed at coaching staff changes.

articles.headline: Footpath drive to cover all five corporations, says DKS
articles.summary: Karnataka Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar announced that the state’s drive against footpath encroachments will expand to all five city corporations. Authorities have already cleared 435 km of footpaths in Bengaluru. Shivakumar emphasized that while street vendors' livelihoods are important, public pedestrian space must be reclaimed. Designated vending zones will be identified to prevent congestion.

articles.headline: Mysuru Dasara to be held over 11 days: CM
articles.summary: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah announced that the Mysuru Dasara festival will be celebrated on a grand scale for 11 days starting in October. The government plans to showcase the state’s diverse cultural traditions and has allocated funds for several new programs. A high-level committee will oversee the preparations to ensure the 'Nada Habba' (state festival) attracts global tourists.

articles.headline: AP to set up STF over social media misuse
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh government, led by CM Chandrababu Naidu, has decided to establish a Special Task Force (STF) to combat social media abuse. The task force will address cyber harassment, misinformation, and online defamation, particularly targeting women and public figures. The cabinet also approved the use of advanced digital forensics to track and prosecute those spreading fake news.

articles.headline: Lokesh invites Korean shipbuilders to invest in Andhra
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh IT Minister Nara Lokesh invited major South Korean shipbuilding firms, including HD Korea Shipbuilding & Offshore Engineering, to invest in the state's 1,000-km coastline. During a meeting in Seoul, Lokesh highlighted the state's strategic location and ease of doing business. The government aims to develop a global shipbuilding hub to boost the blue economy.

articles.headline: ‘Canada’s clean chit on Nijjar vindication of India’s stand’
articles.summary: The Indian government stated that the lack of evidence in the Canadian investigation into the 2023 killing of Hardeep Singh Nijjar vindicates its stance. New Delhi has long maintained that allegations of Indian official involvement were politically motivated. Indian diplomats noted that the focus should instead be on the rise of pro-Khalistan extremism on Canadian soil.

articles.headline: NH projects to be awarded within 6 to 12 months
articles.summary: The Ministry of Road Transport and Highways (MoRTH) has set a target to award all national highway projects that do not require land acquisition or forest clearance within 6 to 12 months. This revised framework aims to accelerate infrastructure development and ensure that projects with ready approvals are not stalled. Projects not awarded within two years will face de-sanctioning.

articles.headline: Assam budget: Benefits for tea growers, hiked MLA fund
articles.summary: Assam's first annual budget under the new BJP-led government features a fiscal deficit of 3% and several populist measures. Highlights include a ₹2,000 crore allocation for tea garden infrastructure, a hike in the MLA Development Fund to ₹5 crore per year, and a VAT cut on piped natural gas. The government also targets 200,000 new employment opportunities over the next five years.

articles.headline: Ruckus in SC as litigant threw papers, abused CJI
articles.summary: Chaos erupted in the Supreme Court when a litigant threw case papers and used abusive language against Chief Justice of India D.Y. Chandrachud during a hearing. Security personnel immediately detained the individual. The bench, expressing concern over the growing lack of decorum in courts, has referred the matter to the Delhi Police for a detailed investigation and potential legal action.

articles.headline: Enhance security at fishing harbours, Shah directs officials
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah has directed the Department of Fisheries and state police chiefs to significantly enhance security at all fishing harbours and landing centers. The move aims to strengthen coastal security following a high-level meeting on maritime threats. A new federal agency for port security is expected to be operational by the end of 2026 to oversee these efforts.

articles.headline: 3-month jail for actor Rajpal in cheque bounce cases
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court upheld a three-month prison sentence for Bollywood actor Rajpal Yadav in a 2010 cheque bounce case. The court rejected Yadav’s plea for further time to pay ₹1.05 crore in compensation, noting he had been given multiple opportunities since 2013 to honor his undertakings. The court stated his lack of merit in his appeal did not justify 'condoning the delay.'

articles.headline: Aero India-2027 to be held in B'luru in Feb
articles.summary: The Ministry of Defence announced that the 16th edition of Aero India will be held at Bengaluru’s Yelahanka Air Force Station from February 8-12, 2027. The biennial show is expected to be the largest yet, showcasing India’s growing indigenous manufacturing capabilities and attracting global defense firms. HAL and BEL have been assigned as lead agencies for the event’s management.

articles.headline: Manipur couple linked to 6 Nagas’ killing held
articles.summary: Manipur Police arrested a couple for their alleged involvement in the killing of six Nagas following a twin ambush in the Kangpokpi and Senapati districts. The arrests were made based on 'credible inputs' regarding their role in the ethnic violence that has plagued the state since May 2023. Over 260 people have died in the conflict, leaving tens of thousands displaced.

articles.headline: Plantation drives matter only if the trees survive
articles.summary: An analysis of India’s massive tree-planting initiatives suggests that while millions of saplings are planted annually, survival rates remain low due to poor post-planting care and a lack of accountability. Experts argue that focus must shift from 'planting' targets to 'growing' forests. The report highlights that heat islands in cities continue to worsen despite these greening efforts.

articles.headline: Heavy rainfall in Himachal; alert issued until July 15
articles.summary: Continuous heavy rainfall in Himachal Pradesh has triggered landslides and flooded major rivers like the Beas, disrupting traffic on over 250 roads. The weather department has issued a high alert for districts including Kullu, Sirmaur, and Solan until July 15. Authorities have advised residents and tourists to avoid traveling near riverbanks and landslide-prone areas.

articles.headline: Bengal CM: New anti-crime law to be enforced July 13
articles.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee announced that a new anti-crime law aimed at controlling organized crime and extortion will be enforced from July 13. The law allows for preventive detention of up to 12 months for repeat offenders and includes provisions for property attachment to compensate victims. The move follows similar legislative steps taken in states like UP and Gujarat.

articles.headline: W Asia tense as mysterious strikes rattle Iran
articles.summary: Tensions in West Asia escalated as unexplained airstrikes hit multiple targets across Iran, including oil facilities in Bandar Abbas. While no country has claimed responsibility, the strikes occurred amid warnings from the US and Israel regarding Iran’s nuclear program. Iran has reportedly put its air defenses on high alert and threatened 'decisive retaliation' against any provocations.

articles.headline: EU warns Instagram, FB to change addictive features
articles.summary: The European Commission has warned Meta that Instagram and Facebook must change 'addictive' features like endless scrolling and push notifications or face significant fines. The EU argues these features exploit children's vulnerabilities and violate consumer protection laws. Meta has been given a deadline to propose changes to ensure user well-being and data transparency.

articles.headline: China follows SpaceX’s lead, recaptures 1st stage of rocket
articles.summary: China successfully recovered the first stage of its Long March-10B rocket following a test launch from Hainan Island. This marks a major milestone in China's effort to develop reusable launch vehicles, mirroring the technology pioneered by SpaceX. The achievement is expected to significantly reduce the cost of satellite launches and support China's future lunar and space station missions.

articles.headline: Markets rally on TCS, softer crude prices
articles.summary: Indian markets ended higher as the Sensex gained over 800 points and the Nifty crossed the 23,500 mark. The surge was led by strong Q1 earnings from TCS and a drop in global crude oil prices, which eased inflation concerns. IT and banking stocks were the primary beneficiaries of the positive sentiment, helping markets recover after a week of volatile trading.

articles.headline: June equity MF inflow rises 26% to ₹28,973 crore
articles.summary: Equity mutual fund inflows in India rose by 26% in June, reaching a total of ₹28,973 crore. Data from AMFI shows that the surge was driven by strong retail participation and a preference for thematic and small-cap funds. Overall industry assets under management (AUM) reached a record high of ₹58.22 lakh crore, reflecting robust domestic liquidity despite global economic uncertainties.

articles.headline: Modi, Albanese launch sports initiatives to strengthen ties
articles.summary: Prime Ministers Narendra Modi and Anthony Albanese launched a new 'Roadmap for Sports Collaboration' between India and Australia. The initiative includes a partnership for 'Women in Sports' and capacity building for the 2032 Brisbane Olympics. The leaders also announced that a Big Bash League game will be organized in Chennai this December to further deepen cricketing ties.

articles.headline: India batters come up short, lose series to England
articles.summary: The Indian women's cricket team lost their third consecutive T20I against England, resulting in a series defeat. Chasing 158, the Indian lineup was restricted to 118 for 9 as England's bowlers utilized short-ball tactics effectively. This marks the first time since 2018-19 that the Indian women have lost five consecutive T20Is at home, raising concerns ahead of the upcoming Asia Cup.

articles.headline: June inflation likely breached RBI target for first time in months
articles.summary: A Mint poll of 18 economists predicts retail inflation reached 4.2% in June 2026, surpassing the RBI's 4% medium-term target for the first time in 18 months. The 30 basis point rise from May is attributed to firmer food prices and the full-month impact of petrol and diesel price hikes. Official data for the new 2024-base series is expected on July 13.

articles.headline: Administrative neglect and lack of grassroots training ail Indian football
articles.summary: Sunil Chhetri’s extraordinary career highlights the paradox of Indian football: a nation with immense passion held back by weak grassroots development and administrative neglect. Despite Chhetri being the world’s fourth-highest international scorer, India remains outside the global top 100 as the domestic league structure faces financial and legal instability.

articles.headline: Panruti jackfruit earns GI tag as gourmet culinary interest grows
articles.summary: The Panruti jackfruit has been awarded a Geographical Indication (GI) tag for its distinctive aroma and sweet taste. Cultivated as a commercial monocrop in Tamil Nadu, the fruit is now being rediscovered by fine-dining chefs as a versatile ingredient for everything from tacos to gelato, moving beyond its traditional status as a 'poor man’s food.'

articles.headline: Students and activists protest education system failures at Jantar Mantar
articles.summary: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) has led a round-the-clock protest at Jantar Mantar for over three weeks following multiple exam paper leaks. Joined by climate activist Sonam Wangchuk on a hunger strike, the protesters are demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and radical reforms to prevent further student stress and suicides.

articles.headline: PSU general insurers back shift to risk-based capital regime
articles.summary: State-owned general insurers are supporting a move to a risk-based capital framework starting April 2027. By valuing assets like equity and real estate at market prices instead of historical costs, struggling firms like Oriental Insurance and United India expect their solvency positions to improve significantly, potentially meeting regulatory thresholds.

articles.headline: Sensex and Nifty rally as TCS results revive investor sentiment
articles.summary: Indian equities recovered on Friday with the Sensex jumping 1.08% to 77,569.38 and Nifty gaining 1.02%. The rebound was driven by Tata Consultancy Services' June-quarter earnings, which exceeded revenue expectations, and easing West Asia tensions. However, benchmarks ended the week slightly lower following mid-week volatility and crude oil price concerns.

articles.headline: Truecaller may legally challenge Trai's directive on spam labelling
articles.summary: Truecaller CEO Rishit Jhunjhunwala stated the firm may challenge a telecom regulator directive that bars labelling calls from the 140 and 1600 series as spam. Truecaller argues the restriction undermines consumer protection by suppressing community feedback. Trai maintains these series are designated for trustworthy transactional and promotional calls.

articles.headline: Government tightens sale of liquid medicines with high alcohol content
articles.summary: The health ministry has moved oral liquid medicines containing over 12% ethyl alcohol into the restricted Schedule H1 category. Formulations like cough syrups and tinctures in bottles larger than 30ml will now require a doctor's prescription for sale. The move aims to curb the misuse of high-alcohol medicinal mixtures for intoxication.

articles.headline: Finance Ministry to strategize with bank heads on foreign inflows
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has convened a meeting with public sector bank chiefs on July 13 to discuss mobilizing foreign capital. The strategy focuses on boosting FCNR(B) deposits and overseas borrowings to expand the funding base of banks, as credit growth continues to outpace deposit growth in the domestic market.

articles.headline: Government mandates CPSEs pay MSME suppliers through TReDS platforms
articles.summary: The MSME ministry has made it compulsory for central public sector enterprises to settle payments to small business suppliers via RBI-approved TReDS platforms. This move, announced in the 2026-27 Union Budget, aims to curb chronic payment delays and improve access to working capital for MSMEs by making CPSEs role models for payment discipline.

articles.headline: Indian manufacturers redesign products to withstand extreme climate risks
articles.summary: From air conditioners to EV batteries, Indian firms are re-engineering products to handle rising temperatures and thermal stress. Innovations include higher-heat-resistant insulation and advanced cooling technologies. Economic losses from climate disasters in India exceeded $12 billion in 2023, prompting companies like Blue Star and JK Tyre to focus on durability.

articles.headline: Beauty brand Plum plans record $100 million institutional fundraise
articles.summary: D2C beauty brand Plum has appointed Rothschild & Co to manage a $75-100 million funding round. If successful, it would be the largest single institutional fundraise for an Indian beauty brand. The proceeds will fund offline expansion and new product launches as the company matures into an omnichannel business after reaching full-year profitability.

articles.headline: SBI digitizes stressed asset monitoring to improve recovery oversight
articles.summary: State Bank of India is developing the 'Stressed Asset Lifecycle Management System' to digitize its legacy bad-loan tracking. Expected to go live by January 2027, the platform will consolidate details of stressed accounts into a single dashboard. This final major business vertical digitization aims to reduce procedural bottlenecks and improve accountability.

articles.headline: European teams dominate quarter-final lineup in expanded World Cup
articles.summary: Six European nations have secured spots in the World Cup quarter-finals, cementing the continent's dominance. Argentina and Morocco stand as the only non-European contenders. The tournament enters its final leg with stars like Mbappe, Haaland, and Messi competing in a high-stakes Golden Boot shootout as goals reach record numbers in the 104-game format.

articles.headline: India wins historic bronze at AVC Men's Volleyball Cup
articles.summary: The Indian men's volleyball team captured a historic bronze medal at the Asian Volleyball Confederation (AVC) Cup in Ahmedabad. Despite administrative turmoil and limited training, India defeated Bahrain 3-1 in the play-off. The win has propelled India’s global ranking to 42, fulfilling the mandate for Asian Games participation.

articles.headline: Invus Group close to buying stake in Popo Ventures
articles.summary: Global private equity firm Invus Group is in advanced talks to acquire a minority stake worth ₹500 crore in Bengaluru-based restaurant operator Popo Ventures. The deal values the company, which owns brands like The Pizza Bakery and Paris Panini, at up to ₹1,350 crore. Organized food services in India are projected to grow at 10-11% CAGR through 2030.

articles.headline: NSE to pitch IPO to 30 global investors this month
articles.summary: The National Stock Exchange of India plans to pitch its initial public offering to over 30 global investors, highlighting the country's rapid financial expansion. The exchange is betting on growing capital-market participation and expects 15% growth in its currency derivatives market as it seeks to list on the public markets.

articles.headline: Zetwerk and Tonbo Imaging receive Sebi approval for IPOs
articles.summary: Contract manufacturing platform Zetwerk and defense imaging firm Tonbo Imaging have received the green light from Sebi to launch their initial public offerings. The approvals come amid a surge in primary market activity. Both Bengaluru-based firms represent high-growth sectors in India's industrial and defense technology landscapes.

articles.headline: Manil Suri’s memoir explores familial bonds in stifling Bombay
articles.summary: Author Manil Suri’s new memoir, 'A Room in Bombay,' offers a candid look at his life growing up in a single sublet room with his parents. The book examines the intense, all-consuming bond with his mother, Prem, and his journey of self-discovery and homosexuality against the backdrop of a co-dependent family life in a crowded Mumbai apartment.

articles.headline: Subhakar Tadi’s art explores political ideologies through urban walls
articles.summary: Artist Subhakar Tadi’s solo show, 'Writings on the Wall,' at Akara Contemporary, uses the wall as a subject to discuss power, protest, and ideology. His photorealistic oil paintings create cinematic urban facades that reflect on news of war, displacement, and the selective empathy of modern society in its reaction to global and local violence.

articles.headline: Shahana Rajani maps shifting Indus delta through community knowledge
articles.summary: Toronto-based artist Shahana Rajani's film 'Lines that World a River' criticizes colonial cartography of the Indus river. On display at Prameya Art Foundation, the work explores how hydraulic engineering attempted to control the river and how current climate change threatens the sacred sites and livelihoods of local fishing communities.

articles.headline: SEBI Considers Virtual KYC to Simplify NRI Investments
articles.summary: Market regulator SEBI is exploring the implementation of virtual KYC and geo-location tagging for Non-Resident Indians (NRIs). This move aims to eliminate the requirement for physical presence at bank branches in India for account opening and investment. The regulator is coordinating with the RBI and registrar agents to standardize digital verification processes.

articles.headline: Raghuram Rajan Joins US Federal Reserve Policy Review Team
articles.summary: Former RBI Governor Raghuram Rajan has been appointed to a high-level Federal Reserve task force to review future policy frameworks. Rajan, along with Indian-origin experts Raj Chetty and Asha Sharma, will assist the US central bank in reviewing policy perspectives and potential structural changes. Rajan is currently a professor at the University of Chicago Booth School of Business.

articles.headline: World Bank Approves $890 Million for India's Rooftop Solar
articles.summary: The World Bank group has approved $890 million in financial aid to accelerate India's rooftop solar energy program. The funding includes $820 million in loans and $70 million in concessional credits and grants. The initiative aims to provide clean energy to millions of households and create approximately 1.7 million jobs in the renewable energy supply chain.

articles.headline: FADA Reports Zero Complaints Regarding E20 Fuel Impact
articles.summary: The Federation of Automobile Dealers Associations (FADA) stated it has not received any written complaints regarding engine damage or rust caused by E20 petrol. FADA represents 30,000 dealers and supports the government’s claim that higher ethanol blending is safe for modern vehicles. The organization has launched a nationwide campaign to address customer concerns about fuel efficiency.

articles.headline: HFCL Secures Rs 495.80 Crore Export Order for Fiber
articles.summary: Domestic telecom equipment maker HFCL has won an export contract worth Rs 495.80 crore from a leading international data center company. The order involves the supply of optical fiber cables for data center connectivity solutions and is expected to be completed by December 2026. This follows the launch of HFCL’s AI-focused data center solution vertical.

articles.headline: REC to Invest Rs 15,000 Crore in Tripura Power
articles.summary: Public sector unit REC Ltd has signed agreements with the Tripura government to provide Rs 15,000 crore in financial assistance for power and renewable projects. The funding includes Rs 5,000 crore for green energy initiatives via TREDA and Rs 10,000 crore for electricity infrastructure modernization through TSECL. The goal is to ensure reliable and sustainable power in the state.

articles.headline: Hindustan Copper Appoints Advisors for Chile Mine Acquisitions
articles.summary: Government-owned Hindustan Copper (HCL) has appointed transaction advisors to facilitate the acquisition of four copper mines in Chile. The company is currently conducting due diligence and studying regulatory data. While HCL officials noted that the process is in early stages and regulatory approvals in both countries take time, the move is a key part of India's strategic mineral expansion.

articles.headline: L&T Finance Reports 31% Profit Growth in Q1
articles.summary: Non-banking financial company L&T Finance reported a consolidated net profit of Rs 916 crore for the quarter ending June 2026, marking a 31% year-on-year increase. The growth was driven by a 27% rise in the consolidated loan book, which reached Rs 1.30 lakh crore. Retail loans grew by 28%, significantly exceeding the company's long-term annual growth target of 20%.

articles.headline: Court Dismisses Jostel's New Plea in Ongoing Oyo Dispute
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has dismissed a new application by backpacker hostel chain Jostel against Oyo's parent company, Oravel Stays. Jostel had sought interim protection for its claimed 7% equity stake in Oyo ahead of its proposed IPO. The court noted that previous binding orders remain in place and scheduled the main appeal hearing for August 12.

articles.headline: Large-Cap Mutual Funds Struggle with Risk-Adjusted Returns
articles.summary: An analysis of mutual fund performance reveals that large-cap schemes are finding it difficult to maintain high Sortino ratios, a measure of return relative to downside risk. Only 21.7% of flexi-cap funds and no active large-cap funds managed a ratio above 1 over a three-year period. While small and mid-cap funds have outperformed recently, experts warn of cyclical shifts in market leadership.

articles.headline: SBI to Sell 1.42% Stake in Funds Management Arm
articles.summary: State Bank of India (SBI) will divest a 1.42% stake in SBI Funds Management (SBIFM) for Rs 1,655 crore via pre-IPO placement. The bank entered into agreements with 30 institutional and large investors at a price of Rs 574 per share. This placement values India's largest asset management company at approximately Rs 1.2 lakh crore ahead of its planned initial public offering.

articles.headline: NSE Crude Oil Options Hit Record Daily Premium Turnover
articles.summary: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) recorded its highest-ever daily premium turnover in crude oil options on July 9, reaching Rs 2,006.49 crore. Over 4.7 million contracts were traded, with open interest exceeding 114,000 contracts. NSE attributed the growth to increased hedging activity by traders seeking to manage price risks in a volatile global energy market.

articles.headline: SAT Adjourns Hearing on SEBI Probe Against Five FPIs
articles.summary: The Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT) has postponed the hearing on appeals filed by five Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) against SEBI's current investigation until July 15. The FPIs, including Asia Investment Corporation and APMS Investment Fund, are challenging SEBI's procedural actions. SEBI argued that delays in the legal process are hindering the timely progress of the investigation.

articles.headline: Redseer: 210 New-Age Companies Ready for IPOs
articles.summary: A report by consultancy firm Redseer estimates that approximately 210 new-age companies will be ready to launch IPOs within the next 24 months. The report projects that the market capitalization of these companies will grow from $150 billion in July to $1 trillion by 2030. Indian domestic institutions have become the primary buyers, favoring companies that demonstrate profitable growth.

articles.headline: Indian Bank and Bank of Maharashtra Post Strong Q1 Profits
articles.summary: Public sector lenders reported robust growth in the first quarter of FY27. Indian Bank's net profit rose 10% year-on-year to Rs 3,272 crore, while Bank of Maharashtra's profit jumped 27% to Rs 2,020 crore. Both banks saw significant increases in net interest income and overall deposits, reflecting strong credit demand and improved operational efficiency.

articles.headline: Finance Minister to Review PSU Bank NRI Deposit Campaigns
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman will meet with heads of Public Sector Banks (PSBs) and IDBI Bank on July 13 to review the progress of foreign currency deposit mobilization. The meeting will focus on the progress of FCNR-B deposits and external commercial borrowings following the RBI's relaxation of interest rate caps for NRI deposits to attract more foreign currency into the country.

articles.headline: MSME Payments via TReDS Platform Made Mandatory for CPSEs
articles.summary: The government has issued revised guidelines making it mandatory for all Central Public Sector Enterprises (CPSEs) to settle MSME bills through the TReDS platform. The move aims to ensure timely payments to small businesses and improve their access to working capital. CPSEs must now provide auditor certificates confirming their registration and compliance with the TReDS system.

articles.headline: India-UK Trade Deal Car Tariffs Dropping to 10%
articles.summary: India has notified concessional import duties for UK cars under the Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement. Tariffs on a specific quota of vehicles will drop from 110% to 30% in the first year and eventually to 10% over five years. This move is expected to benefit premium brands like Jaguar Land Rover and Bentley while maintaining protections for the domestic electric vehicle ecosystem.

articles.headline: Prescriptions Mandated for Medicines with 12% Alcohol Content
articles.summary: The Union Health Ministry has mandated that oral medicines containing more than 12% ethyl alcohol will now require a doctor's prescription. These drugs have been moved to the Schedule H1 category to prevent misuse and hoarding. The new regulation, which becomes effective in January 2027, requires pharmacies to maintain strict sales records and doctor prescriptions for such products.

articles.headline: Editorial: MGNREGA Shifts from Guarantee to Formula-Based Scheme
articles.summary: A policy shift in the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA) has moved it toward a pre-determined allocation model based on fixed parameters rather than actual demand. Critics argue this transition weakens the rural safety net, as states may struggle to provide work during crises once standard limits are reached, potentially forcing more migration to urban areas.

articles.headline: Opinion: Marketing Lessons from Imtiaz Ali's Film Success
articles.summary: Film industry experts highlight the unconventional marketing success of Imtiaz Ali's 'Main Wapas Aaunga,' which turned a low opening into a hit. The strategy focused on direct audience interaction across various cities and leveraged emotional storytelling over digital ads. The success proves that authentic audience connection and a strong product can overcome initial box office failures.

articles.headline: Australia Tightens Student Visa Norms for Indian Applicants
articles.summary: Australia has placed India in the 'high-risk' category for student visa evaluations, along with Nepal and Pakistan. This change does not ban Indian students but leads to more intensive scrutiny of applications and demands for additional documentation. The decision follows Australian concerns regarding fraudulent documents and the rising number of visa misuse cases among international students.

articles.headline: Lack of Jobs Slows Women's Labor Participation in India
articles.summary: EAC-PM Chairman S. Mahendra Dev states that the primary barrier to increasing women's labor participation in India is the lack of suitable jobs, rather than traditional social norms. While social mindset was previously the main hurdle, data now suggests that educated women face high unemployment rates. He emphasized the need for more productive employment opportunities in labor-intensive sectors.

articles.headline: TRAI: 1600 and 140 Series Numbers Cannot Be Blocked
articles.summary: Telecom regulator TRAI has clarified that phone numbers starting with the 1600 and 140 series cannot be blocked or tagged as spam by third-party management apps. These series are reserved for essential service and transactional calls by financial institutions regulated by RBI, SEBI, and IRDAI. The move aims to ensure that critical banking alerts and transaction verifications reach consumers reliably.

articles.headline: India and Australia Launch Roadmap for Sports Cooperation
articles.summary: India and Australia have released a sports cooperation roadmap to enhance training, sports science, and technological exchange. A key highlight includes hosting the opening match of the upcoming Big Bash League (BBL) season in Chennai this December. This marks the first time a major foreign cricket league match will be played in India, aiming to strengthen bilateral sporting and cultural ties.

articles.headline: UN Report: Aid Cuts to Affect 1 Million Women
articles.summary: A UN Women report titled 'Beyond the Breaking Point' warns that funding cuts in official development assistance will leave over 1 million women without essential humanitarian aid by 2025. Only four major donor countries met the target of contributing 0.7% of gross national income to aid. The report highlights severe impacts on women in conflict zones like Afghanistan, Congo, and Haiti.

articles.headline: No Need to Stand for National Anthem Within Film Screenings
articles.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs clarified that the national anthem played as part of a movie or newsreel does not require the audience to stand. The directive aims to prevent disorder during exhibitions. However, the anthem must be played before the film starts, and the audience must stand then. This follows concerns over cinema hall etiquette and legal disruptions.

articles.headline: Duty Cuts to Lower UK Luxury Car Prices in India
articles.summary: Premium automobile brands like Rolls-Royce, Aston Martin, and McLaren are set to become cheaper in India by 1-3 crore as import duties drop from 110% to 30%. The change follows the India-UK free trade agreement taking effect July 15. The lower duty applies to an annual quota of 20,000 vehicles, marking a significant shift for the domestic high-end car market.

articles.headline: Indian AI Startups Raise $1 Billion in First Half of 2026
articles.summary: India's AI-focused startups crossed the $1 billion funding mark in the first six months of 2026, a 33% increase from the previous year. Venture capital firms are doubling down on artificial intelligence, moving from top-tier models to vertical-specific applications. Major investments were led by firms like Sarvam and Agnitio, signaling robust global confidence in India's tech ecosystem.

articles.headline: Oil Ministry Defends E20 Fuel Policy Amid Engine Concerns
articles.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas has defended its ethanol blending policy after claims surfaced regarding engine damage from E20 fuel. Officials stated that while mileage might drop by 3-5%, the fuel does not harm modern vehicle components. The government plans to invest 1 lakh crore annually in ethanol infrastructure to ensure farmer welfare and reduce fuel import bills.

articles.headline: Zetwerk Receives Sebi Approval for 5,000 Crore IPO
articles.summary: Contract manufacturing major Zetwerk has received Sebi's nod for its initial public offering, expected to be valued around 5,000 crore. The IPO will include a fresh issue of shares worth 2,800 crore alongside an offer for sale from existing investors. This marks one of the most anticipated listings in the manufacturing sector, aiming to capitalize on India's supply chain expansion.

articles.headline: MoSPI to Launch Retail Sales Survey for Consumer Spending Patterns
articles.summary: The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation plans to launch a nationwide retail sales survey to gather data on consumer spending patterns. Unlike the Household Consumption Expenditure Survey, this initiative will capture data directly from retail establishments. The survey aims to provide more granular insights into demand for specific categories like electronics and apparel.

articles.headline: iPhone 17 Models See High Demand Ahead of Price Hikes
articles.summary: Apple's iPhone 17 models are witnessing rapid sell-outs in India as consumers rush to buy before anticipated price hikes. Retailers report a surge in demand for base models following news that supply chain costs and import duties may push prices up by 10-15%. Market analysts estimate Apple has already shipped 3.5 million units in the current quarter to meet this unexpected frenzy.

articles.headline: Bank Credit Growth Surges to 18.6% in June
articles.summary: Bank credit in India expanded at its fastest pace in two years, growing 18.6% year-on-year for the fortnight ending June 30. The surge was driven by high demand from both the consumer and industrial sectors. Meanwhile, deposit growth remained lower at 13.3%, prompting banks to increase deposit rates and seek alternative funding sources to manage the widening credit-deposit gap.

articles.headline: Tencent Negotiates Stake in AI Firm Manus
articles.summary: Chinese tech giant Tencent is in talks to become the largest shareholder of AI startup Manus. The move comes as Manus seeks new investment after previous backers were required to divest by the US government. Tencent's interest highlights the intense global competition for advanced AI agents that can autonomously execute complex tasks, despite ongoing geopolitical tech tensions.

articles.headline: GST Panel Protects Buyers from ITC Loss Due to Supplier Defaults
articles.summary: The GST Council's law committee has approved a proposal to protect buyers from losing Input Tax Credit (ITC) if their suppliers fail to deposit taxes. Under the new rule, a buyer's ITC will be protected if they have a valid invoice and the transaction was made through banking channels. This addresses a major pain point for businesses facing penalties for the faults of third-party vendors.

articles.headline: Consumer Goods Makers Cut Discounts as Costs Rise
articles.summary: Makers of consumer goods and retail brands are reducing discounts by 5-10 percentage points as raw material costs and freight charges remain high. Companies are shifting away from aggressive promotions to protect margins, signaling an end to the era of deep markdowns. Retailers report that end-of-season sale discounts are being capped at 40%, compared to 60-70% in previous years.

articles.headline: WhatsApp Username Feature Rollout Paused Over Security Concerns
articles.summary: Meta has put the global rollout of its WhatsApp username feature on hold following concerns from the Indian government regarding cybercrime and identity theft. The feature was designed to allow users to communicate without sharing phone numbers. Meta has assured the Ministry of Electronics and IT that it is working to implement safeguards to prevent misuse by fraudsters.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Concerned Over Four-Year Delay in Maharashtra Murder Trial
articles.summary: The Supreme Court expressed strong disapproval over the slow pace of a murder trial in Maharashtra where only two of 45 witnesses have been examined in four years. The bench noted that the state has a duty to conduct trials efficiently. One accused, in jail since 2022, was granted bail due to the 'glaring gap' between incarceration and judicial progress.

articles.headline: Assam Budget Bans Polygamy for Government Benefits and Jobs
articles.summary: The Assam government, in its first green budget, announced that individuals practicing polygamy will be ineligible for government jobs and state schemes. Finance Minister Ajanta Neog stated that any employee found practicing polygamy without prior permission would face dismissal. The budget also includes a ₹77.4k crore allocation for eco-sensitive energy projects and forest conservation.

articles.headline: Centre Issues New Power Tower Norms to Minimize Forest Diversion
articles.summary: The Union Environment Ministry has issued new guidelines for electricity and telecom towers in forest areas to reduce the ecological footprint. The rules encourage the use of modern pole structures and multi-circuit towers, which could slash the forest area requirement for power lines by up to 20%. The move follows concerns raised by the National Tiger Conservation Authority over wildlife impact.

articles.headline: Australia Backs India's Bid for 2036 Olympic Games
articles.summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has formally backed India's bid to host the 2036 Olympic and Paralympic Games. The support was announced during a sports collaboration event in New Delhi. Both nations will work together to share best practices on establishing high-performance sports centers and training coaches, strengthening the bilateral sports-industry roadmap signed in 2023.

articles.headline: Retailers File Anti-trust Complaints Against Flipkart with CCI
articles.summary: The Forum for Internet Retailers, Sellers and Traders (FIRST) has filed a complaint with the Competition Commission of India against Flipkart. The association alleges that Flipkart is abusing its dominant market position through deep discounting and preferential treatment of select sellers. Over 300 complaints from MSME sellers claim these practices have adversely affected their businesses.

articles.headline: India-Australia Strategic Uranium Deal to Accelerate Energy Transition
articles.summary: A new strategic arrangement allowing Australia to sell uranium to India is expected to boost India's low-carbon energy goals. The deal supports India's target of 100GW of nuclear power capacity by 2032. This cooperation follows the bilateral Economic Cooperation and Trade Agreement and signifies Australia's role as a reliable energy partner in India's industrial and green energy shift.

articles.headline: TCS First Quarter Earnings Ease Market Jitters for IT Sector
articles.summary: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) reported better-than-expected quarterly earnings, leading to a 1% rally in Indian IT stocks. The positive results eased concerns over a global slowdown in tech spending. The Nifty IT index rose 2% following the announcement, helping the broader Sensex and Nifty 50 recover from recent losses sparked by West Asian geopolitical tensions.

articles.headline: Trai Mandates 'Spam' Tag for Promotional Calls via 1600 Series
articles.summary: The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (Trai) has directed that all promotional calls from registered entities using the 140 and 160 series must be clearly tagged as spam. This measure aims to help consumers distinguish between essential financial transaction calls and unsolicited marketing. Trai is also working on a caller-identification feature to combat fraudulent calls.

articles.headline: DGCA Issues Warning to IndiGo Over Dangerous Goods Lapses
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has issued a warning to IndiGo regarding non-compliance with dangerous goods regulations. The regulator's audit found lapses in the airline's training programs and operational manuals. The warning follows a cargo spillage incident earlier this year, and IndiGo has been directed to review its safety procedures and cargo handling protocols.

articles.headline: England to Face Norway in World Cup Quarterfinals
articles.summary: England is set to face Norway in a highly anticipated World Cup quarterfinal match following Norway's victory over the United States. Fans are particularly focused on the duel between Erling Haaland and Jude Bellingham. This match marks a crucial step for England as they aim to end their 60-year wait for a major international title, with both teams showing top form in the tournament.

articles.headline: Messi Leads Argentina to Semi-finals Against Cape Verde
articles.summary: Lionel Messi scored a hat-trick as Argentina defeated Cape Verde 5-2 to advance to the World Cup semi-finals. Despite an early scare where Cape Verde took a surprise lead, the defending champions rallied in the second half. Messi's performance brings his tournament goal tally to eight, keeping him in the lead for the Golden Boot as Argentina moves closer to another title.

articles.headline: SC hears plea of cancer patient listed 57 times before death
articles.summary: The Supreme Court expressed concern after a breast cancer patient died while her plea for an affordable generic version of a life-saving drug was listed 57 times without a final hearing. The court has now appointed an amicus curiae to examine the delay in judicial proceedings for such urgent health-related matters.

articles.headline: NBWL panel opposes 2,000MW Sharavathi pumped storage project
articles.summary: The National Board for Wildlife expert committee has recommended against the proposed 2,000MW Sharavathi Pumped Storage Project in the Western Ghats. The panel warned that the project would threaten the survival of rare and endemic species in the ecologically sensitive region, marking a major setback for the state government's green energy goals.

articles.headline: SIT to probe illegal diversion of FCI rice to ethanol plants
articles.summary: A Special Investigation Team has been formed to probe the illegal diversion of subsidized rice from the Food Corporation of India to ethanol plants in Madhya Pradesh. The investigation follows reports of large quantities of rice meant for welfare schemes being redirected to private mills and sold to ethanol units for profit.

articles.headline: Australia's Big Bash League to open season in Chennai this Dec
articles.summary: For the first time in its history, Australia's T20 Big Bash League will hold its season-opening match in India. The match between defending champions Perth Scorchers and Melbourne Renegades will take place in Chennai this December, following a sports collaboration agreement between the prime ministers of both nations.

articles.headline: K'taka continues to host highest number of international students
articles.summary: According to the latest AISHE report for 2023-24, Karnataka remains the top destination for international students in India, hosting 7,914 individuals. The state accounts for nearly 13.6% of the total foreign student population in the country, followed by Maharashtra and Uttar Pradesh.

articles.headline: State improves in school performance index but scores remain poor
articles.summary: Karnataka's score in the Performance Grading Index (PGI) for schools improved slightly to 595.6 out of 1,000, placing it in the 31-40% bracket. While the state showed progress in infrastructure and governance processes, it continues to lag in learning outcomes compared to top-performing states like Kerala and Punjab.

articles.headline: Bengaluru drive targets 1,581 abandoned cars on public roads
articles.summary: The Greater Bengaluru Authority has identified 1,581 abandoned vehicles across public spaces and launched a drive to remove them. Owners have been given one week to move their vehicles, failing which they will be towed and auctioned. The move aims to clear road space and improve hygiene in the city.

articles.headline: Three-month deadline set to finish Wind Tunnel Junction underpass
articles.summary: Karnataka Minister Krishna Byre Gowda has set an October deadline for the completion of the long-delayed underpass at Wind Tunnel Junction on Old Airport Road. The project, which has faced multiple delays due to utility shifting issues, is 75% complete and aims to provide a signal-free corridor to Whitefield.

articles.headline: Minister says Karnataka cannot release Cauvery water to Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: Water Resources Minister Ramalinga Reddy stated that Karnataka is not in a position to release Cauvery water to Tamil Nadu due to deficient rainfall and low storage levels in the KRS dam. The state currently holds 14.5 tmcft of water, which is barely sufficient for the drinking water needs of Mysuru and Bengaluru for the next few months.

articles.headline: High Court upholds uniform property tax for 5-star hotels
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court has upheld the BBMP's decision to apply a uniform property tax rate for all five-star hotels in Bengaluru. The court rejected a petition from a luxury hotel challenging the tax, ruling that five-star hotels form a distinct category and can be taxed uniformly regardless of their specific location within the city.

articles.headline: China leads reusable rocket race as India's NGLV years away
articles.summary: China has successfully completed a vertical landing test of its reusable rocket booster, moving ahead in the global space race. Meanwhile, ISRO's Next Generation Launch Vehicle (NGLV), which also aims for reusability to lower launch costs, is still several years away from its first demonstration flight.

articles.headline: PM Modi lands in New Zealand for historic bilateral visit
articles.summary: Narendra Modi has become the first Indian Prime Minister to visit New Zealand in 40 years. The visit aims to strengthen bilateral ties in defense, education, and security. Modi is scheduled to hold talks with PM Christopher Luxon and engage with the Indian diaspora in Auckland and Wellington.

articles.headline: US Labor Dept investigates Cognizant over H-1B visa fraud claims
articles.summary: The US Department of Labor has launched an investigation into IT giant Cognizant following whistleblower complaints regarding the abuse of H-1B and PERM visa programs. The probe focuses on alleged wage kickbacks, benching employees without pay, and favoring foreign workers over American citizens in hiring practices.

articles.headline: Dixon Tech to acquire majority stake in Vivo India manufacturing
articles.summary: Dixon Technologies is set to acquire a 49% stake in Vivo's Indian manufacturing unit as part of a new joint venture. This move aligns with the Indian government's push for local ownership in Chinese smartphone companies operating in the country. The partnership aims to boost India's position as a global mobile manufacturing hub.

articles.headline: Study finds India lagging in breast cancer survival rates
articles.summary: A global study on cancer survival rates reveals that India's five-year survival rate for breast cancer is 66.4%, significantly lower than the ~90% seen in developed nations. Experts attribute the lag to late-stage diagnosis and unequal access to quality treatment across different regions and socioeconomic groups in India.

articles.headline: Hasina plans return; 'Will surrender in Dec'
articles.summary: Ousted Bangladesh Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina plans to return from exile in December to face a court sentence. Hasina, currently in India, stated she is ready to surrender despite potential arrest. Her return could sharpen political divisions as the interim government in Dhaka seeks to stabilize the country following student-led protests that ended her 20-year rule.

articles.headline: PM Modi Arrives in New Zealand on Historic Visit
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi arrived in New Zealand for the final leg of his three-nation tour, the first visit by an Indian PM in 40 years. He met with PM Christopher Luxon to discuss bilateral cooperation in trade, defense, and culture. The visit is expected to open new avenues for Indian students and the expatriate community while strengthening diplomatic ties between the two nations.

articles.headline: Sinner, Zverev to bid for Wimbledon title
articles.summary: Defending champion Jannik Sinner will face Alexander Zverev in the Wimbledon final. Sinner defeated Novak Djokovic in straight sets (6-4, 6-4, 6-4) in the semi-final, not facing a single break point. Zverev ended the run of British wildcard Arthur Fery in four sets. This marks Sinner's sixth final of the season as he seeks to retain his world No. 1 ranking.

articles.headline: GBA moves SC seeking time till Dec 31 for polls
articles.summary: The Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA) has approached the Supreme Court seeking an extension until December 31 to conduct civic polls. The GBA cited the ongoing revision of electoral rolls and the need for more time to deploy personnel as reasons for the delay. The court had previously directed that elections be held by August 31, and the new plea challenges that timeline.

articles.headline: NIA files charge sheet against 6 Hurriyat leaders
articles.summary: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) has filed a comprehensive charge sheet against six separatist leaders of the Hurriyat Conference in a 1996 case involving the murder of police personnel in Srinagar. The leaders, including Syed Ali Shah Geelani (deceased) and Zafar Akbar Bhat, are accused of participating in a deep-rooted conspiracy to fuel militancy in Jammu and Kashmir.

articles.headline: Israel shared intel with US about Iran plot to kill Trump
articles.summary: Intelligence reportedly shared by Israel with US authorities suggests an active plot by Iran to assassinate former President Donald Trump. US officials stated that security for Trump had already been increased following the detection of these threats. The plot is viewed as potential retaliation for the 2020 killing of General Qasem Soleimani. Iran has dismissed the reports as 'fake news.'

articles.headline: TCS shifts to outcome-based AI deals to boost value
articles.summary: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) is transitioning its AI service contracts from effort-based models to outcome-based ones. This strategy ensures that clients pay for specific results rather than labor hours. TCS reported a Q1 net profit of ₹12,040 crore, a 4% year-on-year increase. The firm is also using its generative AI platform, AI.Cloud, to deliver up to 15% productivity gains for clients.

articles.headline: France find answers to another tricky test
articles.summary: France secured their place in the World Cup semi-finals with a 2-0 victory over Morocco. Goals from Kylian Mbappe and Ousmane Dembele ensured Les Bleus moved one step closer to defending their title. Morocco, the first African team to reach a semi-final, was praised for their defensive resilience, but Mbappe's pace proved decisive. France will now face the winner of Spain vs Belgium.

articles.headline: Delhi HC Denies Interim Relief to Google in Trademark Case
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court refused to grant interim relief to Google India in a trademark dispute involving sanitaryware company Hindware. The case centers on the use of trademarks as keywords in search triggers. The court's division bench has directed both parties to file written submissions and scheduled the final hearing for July 24 to decide if keyword usage constitutes trademark infringement.

articles.headline: Government Defends E20 Petrol Despite Vehicle Mileage Concerns
articles.summary: The Union government has ruled out reversing the E20 ethanol blending policy despite reports of a 3-5% drop in vehicle mileage. Officials stated that supplying pure E10 petrol alongside E20 is not logistically feasible given the massive infrastructure investments already made. The policy is credited with reducing dependence on imported crude oil despite higher local costs.

articles.headline: ONGC to Build 17.5 Lakh Tonne Strategic Oil Reserve
articles.summary: ONGC has approved the construction of a strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) with a capacity of 17.5 lakh tonnes in Mangaluru, Karnataka. The project will include facilities for both strategic and commercial usage, allowing the company to lease storage space or trade commodities. The expansion of the SPR network is aimed at strengthening India's energy security against global supply disruptions.

articles.headline: India's Forex Reserves Rise to $674 Billion
articles.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves rose by $7.26 billion to reach $674 billion for the week ended July 3. The increase is attributed to RBI's measures to attract foreign capital and valuation gains. Currency assets and gold holdings also saw significant growth, providing a strong cushion against global economic volatility and supporting the rupee's stability in international markets.

articles.headline: Mid and Smallcap Funds Drive June Equity Mutual Fund Inflows
articles.summary: Equity mutual fund inflows surged 26% to 28,000 crore in June, hitting a one-year low in May. Mid and small-cap stocks attracted nearly 40% of the total monthly inflows, contributing 11,692 crore. Despite market volatility and the West Asia crisis, SIP contributions reached record highs, showing strong retail investor resilience in the Indian equity markets.
